name: mc_regime_match_quality_unprecedented_regime
description: Unprecedented-regime proof fixture (vision-audit Critical Rec #2 / math-reaudit Critique #1). The historical candidate pool is a TIGHT cluster: 60 days of low SPY returns (centred at +0.001 decimal, dispersion +/-0.0005) so both kNN features (SPY return, rolling 20d vol) have small variance around a small mean. Today's query point is FAR from this pool: spy_today_return is set so that today's z-scored (return, vol) sits several standard deviations from the candidate-pool centroid in BOTH features. Under the z-score standardization already applied inside run_monte_carlo (AC-1), the squared Euclidean distance from today to each candidate-pool day is approximately the Mahalanobis-style score; the mean squared distance over the K nearest neighbours MUST exceed MC_REGIME_MATCH_CHI2_THRESHOLD (chi2(2)_{0.99} ~= 9.21). The fixture is observable two ways: (a) compute_regime_match_quality returns is_unprecedented=True; (b) the caller's downstream gate (compute_exit_confirmation called with the suppressed prob_beating=None) fires the protective stop instead of being vetoed by an MC opinion drawn from least-bad-fit neighbours.
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history (run_monte_carlo's documented {date:{ticker:{daily_ret}}} contract). The unprecedented-regime distance is constructed structurally: a tight historical cluster (low mean, low spread) PLUS a today query point pushed several candidate-pool standard deviations away in both features. The threshold MC_REGIME_MATCH_CHI2_THRESHOLD ~= 9.21 is the chi-squared 0.99-quantile with df=2 (Mahalanobis multivariate-outlier statistic; Aggarwal 2017 Outlier Analysis sec 2.3); fixture is derived from that reference, not pinned to a producer-computed score.
derivation: Construction: 60-day candidate pool with SPY daily returns alternating +/-0.0005 around +0.001 (z-score mean ~0, candidate std ~0.0005). Today's spy_today_return=5.0 pct (=0.05 decimal) is ~98 candidate-pool stds away on the return feature alone; the today_vol feature is also several stds above the cluster's tiny rolling vol. The post-z-score Euclidean^2 between today and ANY candidate day is much greater than 9.21. The MEAN squared distance over the K=8 nearest candidates is therefore far above the chi-squared threshold. Fixture asserts the inequality, not a specific producer-computed score.
